description:
Practical backlog summary anchor: **Tier 1 → Stable, cache-friendly observation block layout**.

Backlog detail: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 6. Stable, cache-friendly observation block layout**.

Implementation context:
- current injected block is assembled in `packages/pi-om-extension/src/engine.ts`
- lifecycle hook for prompt injection is `packages/pi-om-extension/src/index.ts` (`before_agent_start`)
- prompt guidance and continuation reminder live in `src/prompts.ts`
- formatting/reporting utilities are in `src/format.ts`

What this epic should establish:
- deterministic segmentation between durable observations, active task state, suggested response/next action, and continuation guidance
- minimal churn when content is semantically unchanged
- a layout that is easy to migrate to multiple cache-stable messages later if pi exposes the right hook surface

Useful references:
- backlog doc section 6 plus linked Mastra refs (`processor.ts`, `observational-memory.mdx`)
- local tests for context building and extension injection lifecycle

If pi still only permits one mutable system-prompt string here, preserve the same logical segmentation inside that single injected region.
title: Make the injected OM block stable and cache-friendly
